text:
Market today: Mood is cautious-to-nervous. The real driver is macro skepticism around AI valuations - the BIS (central bank of central banks) is flagging AI-driven market froth, and severe weather disrupting AI infrastructure is a new headline risk. Both hit your chip/tech-heavy book directly.

Your holdings: Three need attention. AVGO is your biggest problem - down 16.4%, largest position, on a SELL signal, and your stop at $336 is still $29 below current price, meaning more pain is possible before protection kicks in. PLTR is down 13.2% on a SELL signal - trim or cut it before losses compound on margin. SPAL is down 16.9% and is a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily - it bleeds even in flat markets. On margin, these three are where your real risk lives right now.

Watch: (1) Whether AI infrastructure/data-center spending narratives crack further on the weather + BIS warnings - AVGO and SMH move on this. (2) SPCX price action - brand-new, very volatile IPO with thin data; margin amplifies any gap-downs.

Bottom line: Cut or trim your two SELL names now - on margin, hope is not a stop-loss.
